description: Role-Based Access Control and Shared TypeScript Types for the Medicano platform

context.dependencies: Sprint 01 must be complete — auth, users, clinics, professionals and redis modules already exist

prompts.name: shared-types
prompts.description: Create the shared TypeScript interfaces package used by both backend and frontend

prompts.name: roles-decorator-and-guard
prompts.description: Create the @Roles() decorator and RolesGuard for role-based access control
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed if they exist and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing decorator, guard, or import. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The Role enum already exists at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts — import from there. Do NOT recreate it.
JwtAuthGuard already exists at apps/api/src/auth/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts — do NOT recreate it.

Create apps/api/src/auth/decorators/roles.decorator.ts if it does not already exist:
- export const ROLES_KEY = 'roles';
- export const Roles = (...roles: Role[]) => SetMetadata(ROLES_KEY, roles);
- Import SetMetadata from '@nestjs/common', Role from '../../common/enums/role.enum'.

Create apps/api/src/auth/guards/roles.guard.ts if it does not already exist:
- @Injectable() class RolesGuard implements CanActivate
- constructor(private readonly reflector: Reflector)
- canActivate(context: ExecutionContext): boolean
  - const requiredRoles = this.reflector.getAllAndOverride<Role[]>(ROLES_KEY, [context.getHandler(), context.getClass()])
  - if (!requiredRoles || requiredRoles.length === 0) return true
  - const { user } = context.switchToHttp().getRequest()
  - return requiredRoles.includes(user?.role)
- Import Injectable, CanActivate, ExecutionContext from '@nestjs/common'
- Import Reflector from '@nestjs/core'
- Import Role from '../../common/enums/role.enum'
- Import ROLES_KEY from '../decorators/roles.decorator'

RolesGuard must always be used AFTER JwtAuthGuard — it depends on request.user being set by JwtStrategy.validate().

If either file already exists with the correct content, make no changes.

prompts.name: apply-rbac-to-controllers
prompts.description: Update existing controllers to use RolesGuard and @Roles() on mutation endpoints
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not remove or duplicate existing logic, only add the RBAC decorators where missing. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The following already exist — import them, do NOT recreate them:
- JwtAuthGuard at apps/api/src/auth/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts
- RolesGuard at apps/api/src/auth/guards/roles.guard.ts (created in prompt 2)
- @Roles() at apps/api/src/auth/decorators/roles.decorator.ts (created in prompt 2)
- Role enum at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts

Update apps/api/src/clinics/clinics.controller.ts:
- Change class-level @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) to @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)
- Add @Roles(Role.CLINIC) to: create(), update(), remove()
- GET endpoints (findAll, findOne) stay without @Roles — any authenticated user can read

Update apps/api/src/professionals/professionals.controller.ts:
- Change class-level @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) to @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)
- Add @Roles(Role.CLINIC) to: create(), update(), remove()
- GET endpoints stay without @Roles

Update apps/api/src/professionals/clinic-professionals.controller.ts:
- Change class-level @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) to @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)
- Add @Roles(Role.CLINIC, Role.ATTENDANT) to: assignProfessional(), removeProfessional()
- getProfessionals() stays without @Roles

Do not change any method signatures, return types, or constructor injections. Only add the guard and decorator imports and method-level decorators.

prompts.name: roles-guard-tests
prompts.description: Unit tests for RolesGuard covering all authorization scenarios
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read apps/api/src/auth/tests/roles.guard.spec.ts if it exists and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing test. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

RolesGuard is at apps/api/src/auth/guards/roles.guard.ts — import it from there.
ROLES_KEY is at apps/api/src/auth/decorators/roles.decorator.ts — import it from there.
Role enum is at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts — import it from there.

Create apps/api/src/auth/tests/roles.guard.spec.ts if it does not already exist:
- Do NOT use @InjectModel or real DB connections
- Mock Reflector with jest.fn()
- Build a helper to create a mock ExecutionContext: ({ getHandler: () => jest.fn(), getClass: () => jest.fn(), switchToHttp: () => ({ getRequest: () => ({ user: { role } }) }) })
- afterEach(() => jest.clearAllMocks())

Tests to include:
- 'should allow access when no roles are required': reflector.getAllAndOverride returns undefined → canActivate returns true
- 'should allow access when required roles array is empty': reflector.getAllAndOverride returns [] → canActivate returns true
- 'should allow access when user role matches required role': requiredRoles = [Role.CLINIC], user.role = Role.CLINIC → canActivate returns true
- 'should allow access when user role is in required roles list': requiredRoles = [Role.CLINIC, Role.ATTENDANT], user.role = Role.ATTENDANT → canActivate returns true
- 'should deny access when user role does not match': requiredRoles = [Role.CLINIC], user.role = Role.PATIENT → canActivate returns false
- 'should deny access when user is undefined on request': request has no user → canActivate returns false

If the file already exists with the correct content, make no changes.
